title: HR Executive
description:
Main Duties and Responsibilities:

Payroll

  • Monthly payroll processing
  • Timely submission of monthly CPF contribution
  • Compile and submit payroll report to outsource Accountant
  • Submit income tax clearance (IRA21) for foreign employees
  • Annual online submission of employee remuneration declaration to IRAS
  • Attend to staff enquiries on pay matters and benefits

Benefits

  • Administer staff medical claims and insurance matters
  • Maintain/Update/Monitor employee leave applications and records

Recruitment

  • Attend to walk-in candidates
  • Application of all Work Passes (Work Permit, S-Pass, Employment Pass, Miscellaneous Pass etc)
  • Arrange successful candidates for pre-employment check-up

Training

  • Enroll staff for training
  • Maintain training records and handle training grants etc

Human Resource Operations

  • Monitor and track employee attendance and leave
  • Upkeep employees’ personal files and database
  • Monitor and renew foreign employee work passes
  • Review and revamp existing HR forms
  • Monitor and process new staff confirmation appraisal (for executive and below)
  • Prepare letters of Employment Certification etc
  • Any other duty assigned by the Management

Entry Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma in Human Resource Management or equivalent with at least 3 years of relevant experience
  • Must be well-verse with Employment Act and good HR practices
  • Must be numerate and able to manage confidential information with integrity
  • Strong  team player with good communication and interpersonal skills and ability to relate with all levels of staff
  • Strong organising skill with a problem-solving attitude
  • Proactive and independent with positive work attitude
  • Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint
  • Able to work on weekends, flexible to work occasionally on public holidays and evenings
  • Able to commence work immediately is preferred
  • Please note that only selected candidates will be notified

postedCompany.description:
Singapore Buddhist Sutra Distribution Centre was founded on 16 July 1933 with the aim of promoting the Buddha's teachings and circulation of Buddhist scriptures.  It was renamed The Singapore Buddhist Lodge (SBL) on 17 June 1934, after a series of expansions and change.

To help lay Buddhists learning Dharma and protecting Triple Gems, to propagate Mahayana Buddhism to be relevant to today's world, by following these key cultivations to reach our common goal, the Pureland.

SBL's objective is to propagate Dharma and to perform charitable undertakings.  Since its establishment, SBL has been very active in areas such as promoting Dharma's teaching; reaching out to the destitute, giving out bursaries; provide affordable medical services and etc. to help the general public accumulate merits.
postedCompany.name: Singapore Buddhist Lodge, The
